title: rubus schleicheri subsp. humifusus
intro: Rubus schleicheri subsp. humifusus, also known as the creeping wild raspberry or creeping dewberry, is a subspecies of shrub native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its thorny stems and edible fruits. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits associated with Rubus schleicheri subsp. humifusus include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed fresh or dried, and are often used in traditional medicine to support overall well-being.
